content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SACRAMENTO                           THE HARTFORD BUILDING
1225 EIGHTH STREET, SUITE 550        650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814         SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752            TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800


                                                    FILE NUMBER
                                                    [illegible]


                            April 5, 1984


Janis Shank McLean, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

                Re:  Valuation of Non-Campaign Fundraising
                     Tickets Provided to Public Officials

Dear Ms. McLean:

          Pursuant to our phone conversation of April 4,
1984, I wish to restate the advice you gave me.

          I described a situation in which Corporation A
has purchased a table of tickets to a non-campaign
fundraising event.  Examples would be YMCA Man of the Year
Dinner, California Journal/Coro Foundation California Roast.
Public Official B has been invited as the guest of Corporation
A to that event.  The question is whether or not Corporation
A has made a gift to Public Official B.

          You advised me that it is the opinion of the FPPC
Staff that the value of the ticket to a non-campaign fund-
raiser is not the donation price, but the actual cost of
the meal or beverages consumed.  Therefore, if Corporation A
invites Public Official B to sit at its table at an event,
a gift, as defined in Government Code Section 82028, has been
made by Corporation A and accepted by Public Official B of
the actual value received and therefore Public Official B
has reporting responsibilities under Government Code Section
87207(a)(1) by reason of his attendance at the event, if that
gift and others provided by Corporation A amount to $50 or
more in a calendar year.

Janis Shank McLean, Esq.
April 5, 1984
Page two


     Please confirm our conversation by signing the
attached letter copy and returning it to me.  If I have
misstated any part of our conversation, please advise me
at your earliest convenience.

                                        Kind regards,


                                        Kirk Alan Bessner
                                        Administrator
                                        Political/Reports Unit

KAP:ddl
Attachment


Confirmed by:


_________________________________
JANIS SHANK MCLEAN, ESQ.

sections.question: The question is whether or not Corporation A has made a gift to Public Official B.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether providing a non-campaign fundraising event ticket to a public official constitutes a gift requiring disclosure under Government Code sections 82028 and 87207.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter from an attorney documenting prior telephone advice from FPPC staff. The letter restates the FPPC Staff's opinion but is technically an incoming request/correspondence rather than an official FPPC advice letter response. The FPPC staff member's signature confirmation line appears at the bottom but no actual response is provided in this document. Document discusses meal/beverage valuation for gift analysis under Gov. Code §82028 and §87207(a)(1).

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The question is whether or not Corporation A has made a gift to Public Official B.

embedding.summary: A law firm writes to FPPC staff member Janis Shank McLean to confirm and document telephone advice given on April 4, 1984, regarding whether tickets to non-campaign fundraising events provided by a corporation to a public official constitute gifts subject to reporting requirements.
